# ghflow_pr_merge (Resource)

Merges a pull request. This is a one-shot, irreversible action: all inputs force replacement, and `destroy` only removes the resource from state — it cannot un-merge a PR.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `number` (Number) Number of the pull request to merge.
- `owner` (String) Owner of the repository (user or organization).
- `repository` (String) Name of the repository.

### Optional

- `commit_message` (String) Extra detail appended to the merge commit message (ignored for `rebase`).
- `commit_title` (String) Title for the merge commit (ignored for `rebase`).
- `merge_method` (String) Merge method: `merge`, `squash`, or `rebase`. Defaults to `merge`.
- `required_head_sha` (String) If set, the merge only succeeds when the PR head matches this SHA (passed as the GitHub `sha` parameter). Guards against merging a branch that moved since plan time.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier, set to the merge commit SHA.
- `merge_commit_sha` (String) SHA of the resulting merge commit.
- `merged` (Boolean) Whether the merge succeeded.
